pattern: Already-satisfied customers form a fixed base; a sliding window of fixed width finds the block of grumpy minutes whose recovered customers add the most on top.
hints: Customers during non-grumpy minutes are always satisfied, regardless of where the technique is used; count those separately.
The technique only rescues customers that fall in grumpy minutes inside the chosen window, so maximize the grumpy-minute customers covered by a window of the given width.
Sum the always-satisfied customers, then slide a window of the given length tracking the grumpy-only customers inside it, and add the best window total to the base.

edge_inputs.description: single grumpy minute recovered by the technique
best window is at the end, not the front
zero customers in some grumpy minutes shifts the best window

stress.description: large day with random grumpiness and a random window width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    customers = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    grumpy = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    minutes = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [customers, grumpy, minutes]
stress.note: window width never exceeds n by construction
